title: Koeleria pulchella
taxonomy: Koeleria pulchella is classified within the family Poaceae. This family includes various types of grasses. Koeleria pulchella itself is a species of grass, specifically within the genus Koeleria. The genus Koeleria is part of the order Poales. uses: Koeleria pulchella, also known as purple koeleria, has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. intro: Koeleria pulchella, also known as Purple Hairgrass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and fine, hair-like leaves. The health benefits of Koeleria pulchella include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria pulchella include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Koeleria pulchella is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its health benefits.
